summary: Set 035 Session 3 of 4 — documentation alignment + Layer-3 Playwright coverage. Four deliverables. (1) docs/session-state-schema.md Cancel / restore section finalized: Session 1 had rewritten the lede + bucketing-list bullet; Session 3 added three new subsections — 'Canonical reader' (readCancellationState API table + wired-in caller at fileSystem.ts:readSessionSets), 'Writer symmetry' (TS + Python writer parity citation from Session 2 + cancel/restore symmetric-writes contract + re-cancel preCancelStatus preservation invariant added inline per Round A nice-to-have), 'Layer-3 coverage' (cites cancellation-state-file.spec.ts + summarizes the 3 scenarios). (2) docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md aligned: 'Cancelling and restoring a session set' section reframed state-file-first — 'How the operator triggers it' bullets now name canonical-writer flips on state.json + CANCELLED.md as the both-writes operation, hand-edit affordance points at session-state.json with markdown audit entry recommended-not-required, and explicitly notes hand-dropping only a CANCELLED.md does NOT flip the bucket; 'Detection precedence' rewritten as a three-tier ladder matching fileSystem.ts:readSessionSets (state.status='cancelled' first, non-cancelled status falls through to normal status ladder with no marker override, legacy fallback fires only on no usable state file + CANCELLED.md present with console.warn); RESTORED.md-audit-only paragraph updated to use 'complete' instead of 'done'. Step 1 'Identify the Active Session Set' bullet 'status: complete or CANCELLED.md present = skip' replaced with 'status: complete or status: cancelled = skip'; file-presence retirement explanation tightened. (3) cancelLifecycle.ts JSDoc polish: header comment reframed (markdown markers as durable audit artifacts post-Set-035, state file canonical); byte-equivalence pin cites Session 2's verified parity table (10 rows); prependEntry JSDoc rewritten (audit-history preservation, not filename-presence-detection); cancelSessionSet JSDoc adds writer-symmetry rationale paragraph; restoreSessionSet JSDoc clarifies why isCancelled is still the right file-presence precondition (writer needs file to rename); restoreSessionSet body 'Sequence' comment rewritten for both readers (canonical state-file-first + legacy fallback). Typecheck clean. (4) cancellation-state-file.spec.ts authored — 3 Layer-3 scenarios: state-file-only cancellation (cancelSet then delete CANCELLED.md → data-state=cancelled, exercises readCancellationState='cancelled' branch); legacy fallback (cancelSet then delete state file → data-state=cancelled, exercises 'unknown' + isCancelled(dir) branch); state-file wins (driveHappyPath through 1-session set to status=complete + stray CANCELLED.md → data-state=complete, NO Cancelled bucket header). All 3 green after rebuilding dist/ via node esbuild.js (the extension's package.json points main at dist/extension.js, while tsc only updates out/ — surfaced during initial test execution; documented in step 4). Pre-existing failures: 3 unrelated Layer-3 scenarios in session-sets-tree.spec.ts (ARIA tree structure / orchestrator block provider sublabel / empty-state CTA fallback) trace back to Session 1's empty-state grey-gauge removal in OrchestratorAccordion.ts; out of scope for Session 3 (cancellation docs + Layer-3); deferred to Set 034 styling iteration or hot-fix. Verification: Round A via gemini-pro VERIFIED, no must-fix issues ($0.0218; bundle 48,118 chars). One nice-to-have (re-cancel preCancelStatus preservation documentation) addressed inline. Round B not needed. Cumulative routed Set 035 spend: $0.0612 of $0.50 forecast. Next session (Session 4 of 4): final test sweep + change-log.md aggregation + version bumps + dual-registry release.

reason.specifics: Session 4 (final test sweep + change-log.md aggregation + version bumps + dual-registry release) is a hygiene + release session that builds directly on the documentation + Layer-3 work from Session 3 and the writer-parity contract from Session 2 and the reader migration from Session 1. Same engine + identity keeps the H2 architectural model continuous through the release boundary. Session 4 will also need to surface the 3 pre-existing session-sets-tree.spec.ts failures from Session 1's empty-state grey-gauge removal as either a Session-4 hot-fix or a Set 034 deferral — that decision benefits from the same orchestrator that observed them in Session 3.
